items.title: Canberra childcare centre closes suddenly, worker sacked

children.content: Parents at Edge Early Learning in Higgins received an email last night advising the regulator had ordered the centre to immediately shut.

items.title: Canberra United FC finally has a buyer. Who are the new owners?

children.content: The new owners of the national capital's A-League Women side have pointed to the launch of a men's team.

image.altText: Football teammates celebrating.

items.title: Hume Highway reopens after truck driver dies in crash

children.content: A man has died after a fiery crash on the Hume Highway this morning, where two trucks collided and left part of the highway closed for hours.

image.altText: A plume of smoke behind a fire truck on a road in the dark

items.title: Former diplomat blames Telstra landline service for repeated Triple Zero failures

children.content: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

gallery.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

items.title: ACT minister responds to claim that hospital turned NSW patient away

children.content: ACT Health Minister Rachel Stephen-Smith says a misunderstanding led a NSW patient to believe they had been denied healthcare in Canberra because they lived over the border.

image.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

items.title: Driver charged over fiery multi-truck crash near Goulburn

children.content: A 28-year-old semi-trailer driver is charged with negligent driving over an explosive collision that saw six trucks destroyed in a fire at a rest stop near Goulburn.

image.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

items.title: Man denied assisted dying wins partial victory in early test of ACT law

children.content: A tribunal has ruled on a man's bid to overturn his rejection from the ACT's assisted dying scheme in one of the first tests of how people can challenge such decisions.

image.altText: A building sign that reads 'ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal'.

items.title: Former ACT politician to stand trial on grooming charges

children.content: Former ACT Attorney-General Gordon Ramsay is committed to stand trial for allegedly grooming a teenage boy between 2022 and 2025.

image.altText: Gordon Ramsay walks outside the court in a suit and tie.

items.title: ANU head apologises for failing to protect Jewish staff, students

children.content: The interim vice-chancellor of the Australian National University has apologised for the institution's failure to protect the psychological safety of Jewish students and staff during pro-Palestinian encampments in 2024.

gallery.altText: Tents set up with buildings in the background on the ANU campus.

thumbnail.altText: Tents set up with buildings in the background on the ANU campus.
A woman with long blonde hair wearing a black blazer stands in a conference room looking serious.

items.title: Eight killed, dozens missing as landslide buries people in China

children.content: A landslide in south-western China's Chongqing municipality has buried people in rubble and destroyed a roadway and a number of buildings, according to state media.

image.altText: Large boulders lie across a roadway amid the wreckage of buildings by a mountainside.

items.title: Taking violence seriously is more complex than a royal commission

children.content: Experts are divided about whether there should be a royal commission into violence against women but say the solutions to the problem are already known and need funding.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit with a coat, a red tie and glasses.

avatar.alt: A white woman with red hair wearing a blue dress in front of parliament house

items.title: Hanson laments end of White Australia policy on Tommy Robinson podcast

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has blamed the end of the White Australia policy for migration issues in the country during a controversial podcast appearance with a far-right activist in the UK.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson speaks in the Senate.

items.title: Crows get vital win over Swans to hit top four

children.content: The Adelaide Crows beat the Swans in a thriller in the wet at the SCG to jump into the top four.

gallery.altText: An Adelaide Crows forward grins as he holds his arms out in celebration.

thumbnail.altText: An Adelaide Crows forward grins as he holds his arms out in celebration.

items.title: Melbourne's injury crisis deepens as Warbrick's season ends in loss to Roosters

children.content: Will Warbrick's Melbourne Storm career appears to be over, with the departing winger rupturing his Achilles in his side's 14-6 loss to the Sydney Roosters.

image.altText: A rugby league player in white is driven off the field in a motorised cart, while being hugged by a teammate

items.title: Universities were told of risks from violent intruders at campus protests

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion has heard the tertiary sector's peak standards body wrote to universities to warn them about aggressive outsiders infiltrating pro-Palestinian protests.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder length brown hair, dark rimmed glasses and a black jacket sits behind a microphone.

items.title: 'Carry on patriots': Trump officials defend pilot after fighter jet stunt

children.content: Officials, including US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, continue to support low-altitude military flyovers despite growing safety concerns after a jet was filmed buzzing a crowded beach.
Trump administration officials have backed the manoeuvre, with the White House posting a cartoon of the stunt along with the words "it's okay to love America".
Similar flights have previously come under review but were dismissed by the Pentagon.

articles.title: ICE agent involved in fatal shooting violent and unstable, family says

children.content: Close relatives of the ICE agent understood to have killed a Colombian man in the US this week say he has struggled with mental health issues for most of his life.

image.altText: Blood is seen on the pavement near the scene of a shooting involving ICE.

articles.title: Smoke from Canada wildfires crosses into US prompting warnings to stay indoors

children.content: Heavy, pungent wildfire smoke darkens skies in the US from the Great Lakes to parts of the East Coast, reducing visibility and prompting warnings that breathing the air outside could be dangerous.

image.altText: A pedestrian crosses a street, as the high-rise skyline in the background is obscured in a grey haze.

items.title: NSW woman arrested as part of Dezi Freeman investigation

children.content: A NSW woman was arrested in Bairnsdale today as part of Victoria Police's investigation into the movements of dead police killer Dezi Freeman.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman July image

items.title: Alleged paedophile taught in Darwin area school as recently as last year

children.content: The ABC can reveal a man charged with sexually abusing a "very young child" at a greater Darwin school holiday program had also recently worked at a Top End school.

image.altText: Generic classroom with art on the walls

items.title: Toilet germs and other things living in your make-up brushes

children.content: Various studies have shown that make-up tools, including brushes, can harbour a range of bacteria, including opportunistic pathogens.

gallery.altText: woman hands over jar of makeup brushes

thumbnail.altText: woman hands over jar of makeup brushes

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: Will Ferrell channels Ricky Bobby in TV return

children.content: Plus a noir mystery set in outback Coober Pedy, a true crime drama which doesn't exploit its victims, and more new TV to watch this month.

image.altText: A man in a visor exits a bus

items.title: Decade-long friendship between Roy Orbison's hit maker and Australian singer

children.content: Every Monday morning for the last 14 years, Australian musician Damien Leith has been meeting with the late Joe Melson, the Nashville songwriter responsible for some of Roy Orbison's biggest hits.

image.altText: Damien plays a guitar while sitting in a back car seat next to Joe Melson, who wears a black coat.

items.title: Man jailed after stepfather's body 'left to rot' for 14 months

children.content: Nikola Golem lied to his "mentally unwell" mother about her husband's death for more than a year, and let her sleep beside the decomposing body, Brisbane's Supreme Court heard.

gallery.altText: A man looks into the camera in a suit

thumbnail.altText: A man looks into the camera in a suit

avatar.alt: A portrait shot of Eden standing next to a tree.

items.title: Melbourne, Sydney most expensive in world for beer and cigarettes, study finds

children.content: The Mapping the World's Prices report, published by Deutsche Bank, compares cost-of-living data in 69 cities across six continents.

image.altText: A male hand holding a smoking cigarette, in front of dark background.

items.title: Treasurer says AI key to raising productivity, lowering interest rates

children.content: The Treasurer has given an insight into his April meeting with Anthropic, arguing that Australia embracing AI is crucial for turning around the nation's lacklustre productivity and economic performance over recent decades.

image.altText: A tight shot of Jim Chalmers head, he wears a suit and red tie stands in front of a blue curtain

items.title: US strikes bridges and collapses tower at port in Iran campaign

children.content: The United States expanded its air strike campaign against Iran early Friday by hitting more bridges and apparently collapsing a tower at a key Iranian port.

gallery.altText: Night-time vision - shot from above - shows smoke rising from a road.

thumbnail.altText: Night-time vision - shot from above - shows smoke rising from a road.

items.title: Australia's number one song now has AI credits on Spotify

children.content: DJ and music producer Josh Fawaz has updated the song credits on Spotify on his viral track Like a Prayer. Generative AI drums and vocals are now credited.

image.altText: Josh Fawaz standing in a club setting throwing up two peace signs

items.title: Ex-Wagga MP Daryl Maguire found guilty of conspiracy over fraudulent visas

children.content: After five days of deliberation in the NSW District Court the former Member for Wagga Wagga was found to have conspired to provide false or misleading information to the Commonwealth on visas for Chinese nationals seeking permanent residency.

image.altText: Two men in an official looking board room shake hands in front of audience of onlookers.

items.title: UK urges FIFA to act after Argentine players hold up Malvinas banner

children.content: The British government urges FIFA to investigate after Argentine players held up a banner declaring the Falkland Islands were Argentinian.

articles.title: When is the World Cup final? All you need to know about Spain vs Argentina

children.content: The World Cup final is nearly upon us, with Spain to face Argentina for the ultimate prize. Here's everything you need to know about the biggest game in football.

image.altText: Argentina and Spain badges either side of a 2026 World Cup logo

items.title: NZ detects first case of bird flu in native bird

children.content: Days after a migratory bird tested positive for bird flu in Wellington, authorities have detected a second case of the deadly virus in a native bird.

image.altText: A bird of prey raises its wings as it launches out of a bush.

items.title: Retired teacher says year 12 marks are being inflated in 'unfair' grading system

children.content: A retired South Australian teacher says some educators are inflating the grades of year 12 students, which she says is making the marks "unfair".

image.altText: A teacher in front of a classroom, seen from behind.

items.title: Relief for marine experts as whale helped out of shallow estuary

children.content: A juvenile southern right whale has been saved by a pod of dolphins after it was stranded in an estuary in WA's south for two weeks.

gallery.altText: A southern right whale swimming in green water surrounded by a pod of dolphins

thumbnail.altText: A southern right whale swimming in green water surrounded by a pod of dolphins

items.title: More struggles for Aussie golfers at The Open as shock leader emerges

children.content: Australia's golfers struggle again at The Open as an unlikely American holds the lead.

image.altText: A golfer looks frustrated after missing a putt.

items.title: How motherhood has changed things for BMX racer Lauren Reynolds

children.content: Lauren Reynolds is embracing her tag as one of the most experienced racers at this year's UCI BMX World Championships in Brisbane, her first as a mother.

image.altText: Lauren Reynolds with Royce on her handlebars

items.title: Ultra-low tide makes 1km walk to island lighthouse possible

children.content: A rare chance to walk 1 kilometre offshore to an island lighthouse attracts thousands of trekkers to a North Queensland town.

gallery.altText: People walk across shallow water towards a rocky island with a lighthhouse on top.
A group of people walk through ankle deep water on their way to an island.
People walk up a steep r0cky island towards a lighthouse.

thumbnail.altText: People walk across shallow water towards a rocky island with a lighthhouse on top.
A group of people walk through ankle deep water on their way to an island.
People walk up a steep r0cky island towards a lighthouse.
